title: croton longibracteatus
intro: Croton longibracteatus, also known as the 'Nagara' or 'Dwarf Croton', is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family, Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various alkaloids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Croton longibracteatus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, with the leaves and roots being commonly used in traditional medicine.
